title: rubus villicaulis subsp. ornatus
intro: Rubus villicaulis subsp. ornatus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan raspberry vine, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it effective in treating various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rubus villicaulis subsp. ornatus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, while also providing antioxidant and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, capsules, or topical creams, offering a range of therapeutic options for its medicinal properties.
